stories.title: Charles Schwab Challenge Preview — Åberg, Fowler, Smalley, Thomas at Hogan's Alley
stories.angle: Without Scheffler and McIlroy, this is the widest-open week on the PGA Tour schedule in months — and there are at least four compelling storylines for who takes the title.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
The Charles Schwab Challenge starts TOMORROW at Colonial CC.

No Scheffler. No McIlroy. Wide open.

Åberg: +900, 3 SG/round — best by a mile.
Fowler: hot streak, T2 Truist.
Smalley: T2 PGA, momentum machine.

Hogan's Alley delivers.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

article.headline: Charles Schwab Challenge Preview: Åberg, Fowler, Smalley Lead Wide-Open Colonial Field

stories.title: LIV Golf Korea Preview — Korean Golf Club Plays at Home (May 28-31)
stories.angle: LIV's Korea return with a home team playing in front of Korean fans adds an emotional and commercial dimension rarely seen in LIV's global tour stops.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf Korea starts TOMORROW at Asiad CC in Busan.

The former Iron Heads GC is now Korean Golf Club —
Ben An and Min-kyu Kim added for the home crowd.

DeChambeau defends. 4Aces lead teams. Island green par-3 awaits.

#GolfFanrecap #LIVGolf #LIVKorea

article.headline: Korean Golf Club Comes Home: LIV Golf Korea Preview — May 28-31, Asiad CC Busan

stories.title: Åberg's Win Drought — The Best Player on Tour Not Winning
stories.angle: The win drought is one of the most compelling storylines in golf right now — a generational talent who keeps coming close but keeps finding a way to not finish the job.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Ludvig Åberg averages nearly 3 SG/round — 1+ better than anyone else in the Schwab field.

No wins since the Genesis Invitational.

"Trips over himself on Sundays."

Colonial is his moment. Will he close?

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

article.headline: The Åberg Paradox: Best Player at Colonial — Still No Win Since Last Season

stories.title: US Women's Open — Korda, Thitikul, and a Historic Riviera Debut
stories.angle: A historic venue hosting a major championship for the first time, with the world's best women's player trying to add a second US Women's Open to her career — the storylines are exceptional.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The US Women's Open is June 4-7 at Riviera CC.

First time USGA has ever held a women's major at Riviera.
1,897 entries. All 25 world top-ranked players confirmed.

Korda: 3 wins in 2026. Clear favorite.
World No. 1 Thitikul also entered.

#GolfFanrecap #USWomensOpen #LPGA

article.headline: Riviera Comes to Women's Golf: The 2026 US Women's Open Preview

stories.title: Alex Smalley's Transformation — From Iron Player to Complete Golfer
stories.angle: Smalley's short game transformation makes him one of the most interesting Colonial contenders — a player who was always elite off the tee and on approach, but now has the scoring touch to close.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Alex Smalley lost 0.62 SG around the greens per round in 2024.

In 2026? He's GAINING 0.45 per round.

A 1.07-stroke short game flip. Two T2s. Six straight top-25s.

He arrives at Colonial with the best momentum in the field.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

article.headline: The 1.07-Stroke Flip: How Alex Smalley Fixed the One Thing That Was Holding Him Back
